Life Reflection Math Physics

Repost: life update

January 13, 2026

Scioly happened, and I did really good on it. I did soo good, that if I list what I got, I become identifiable, so just know that for my new year's goals, I have earned 2 points after getting the silver tier. It was far better than the requirements though. And all of that after one day of preparing.

Solid, right? but there's always evil if there's good. For example, My USAMTS! Yeah I absolutely got cooked. I solved p1, reviewed it couple of times, and p1, if you didn't know, is like a free question. And that 5 points I can get from solving p1, is really important for me because I can't really solve much problems correctly. p2. I solved it, and hopefully getting a 5 on it. p3. I didn't solve it. didn't understand the question, didn't even attempt to solve. p4. I partially solved it. expecting like 2 points. more info p5. I could have said something like all primes satisfy to gain particial credits, but I forgot that I wanted to do that, and ended up only submitting p1, p2, and p4. It seems like r1 would be my best round, which is unfortunate. I shouldn't really procrastinate on this, but I did, and this is how it ended up. HM was doable, but it's always these stupid mistakes in every round that trips me. Now hm is basically gone.

I spent time doing some physics, still far from finishing ch 17. Next week is school midterms, and I have 5 midterms of notorious difficulty, so I'm not sure if I'd be able to do much physics. Or I would just fail my midterms. Can't decide what to do honestly. My plan is to finish til ch 17 by end of the next week. It is much later than planned, so I gotta hurry up. I really don't want to fail my midterms. Need at least A- in everything, preferably A in everything, doesn't matter though. But physics is getting quite challenging honestly. I feel like I'm going to get cooked if I don't fully understand everything, and that's part of the reason why I can't speed ahead. There's always some problem that brings in such random stuff and confuse me for a while. Also, I only do odd numbered problems, so imagine where I would be at if I did every problem.

anyways that's it, and I will be going back to studying physics.

Comments
